name: Module Load
description: When a process or program dynamically attaches a shared library, module, or plugin into its memory space. This action is typically performed to extend the functionality of an application, access shared system resources, or interact with kernel-mode components.

event_logs.description: Logs when a DLL is loaded into a process.
Captures process creation events, often useful for correlating module loads.
Can provide visibility into suspicious module loads.

audit_framework.description: Captures when shared libraries (.so files) are loaded.

tracing_tools.description: Monitors process behavior, including library calls.

capabilities: Provide real-time telemetry on module loads and process injections.

tools.name: Sysinternals Process Monitor (procmon)
tools.description: Captures loaded modules and their execution context.

tools.name: Volatility Framework
tools.description: Detects injected DLLs and anomalous module loads.

actions: Centralized log aggregation to correlate suspicious module loads across the environment.
Detection rules using correlation searches and behavioral analytics.
